title: Secure access to physical and digital assets using authentication key
abstract: Authorized access to a digital asset is obtained by associating an authentication tag with a physical object accessible to a user, by configuring the tag with a first datasetcomprised of a random distribution of three-dimensional elements and with a second dataset comprised of machine-readable data elements, and by authorizing a mobile device to scan the elements. The first and second datasets together comprise an authentication key that uniquely identifies the object and, in turn, the user. The authentication key is scanned by a device in response to a prompt from the digital asset to obtain scanned key data. Predetermined key data and a device identifier indicative of the authorized device are stored in a database. Access to the digital asset is allowed when the scanned key data matches the stored predetermined key data, and when the device scanning the data is authorized.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS: 1. A method of securing authorized access to a digital asset, comprising: associating an authentication tag with a physical object accessible to a user; configuring the tag with a first dataset comprised of a random distribution of three-dimensional elements, and with a second dataset comprised of machine-readable data elements, the first and second datasets together comprising an authentication key that uniquely identifies the object and, in turn, the user; authorizing a mobile device to read the authentication key; storing key data indicative of the authentication key in a database; storing a device identifier indicative of the authorized device in the database; scanning the authentication key with the authorized device in response to a prompt from the digital asset to obtain scanned key data; sending device data indicative of the authorized device that is scanning the authentication key to the database; determining if the scanned key data matches the stored key data, and if the sent device data matches the stored device identifier; and allowing access to the digital asset when bom the scanned key data matches the stored key data, and when the sent device data matches the stored device identifier. 2. The method of claim 1, wherein the scanning is performed by configuring the mobile device as a portable, handheld, image capture device, and by aiming the image capture device at the tag to capture return light from the three-dimensional elements; wherein the determining is performed by processing the captured return light from the three- dimensional elements to obtain an image pattern; wherein the stored key data includes an authentication pattern signature indicative of the captured return light from the three- dimensional elements; wherein the data elements are light-reflective and encoded with an 

 address identifier uniquely associated with the authentication pattern signature; wherein the scanning by the image capture device also captures return light from the data elements; and wherein the determining is further performed by decoding the address identifier, by locating the authentication pattern signature associated with the decoded address identifier, and by comparing the image pattern with the located authentication pattern signature. 3. The method of claim 2, wherein the stored authentication pattern signature is colored, wherein the configuring of the tag is performed by coloring the three-dimensional elements, wherein the processing of the return light captured from the three-dimensional elements obtains a colored image pattern, and wherein the comparing is performed by matching the colored image pattern with the colored authentication pattern signature. 4. The method of claim 3, and creating a session code with session parameters in response to an access request received by the digital asset, temporarily storing the session code in the database, capturing the session parameters by the authorized device before the key data is scanned and the device data is sent to the database, determining if the captured session parameters matches the session parameters for the digital asset, and allowing access to the digital asset when the scanned key data matches the stored key data, as well as when the sent device data matches the stored device identifier, as well as when the captured session code parameters matches the session parameters for the digital asset. 5. The method of claim 4, and storing geolocation coordinates of the authorized device and authorized times of operation in the database; configuring the stored session code as at least one of the group consisting of random numbers, time of generation of the session code, and website address of the digital asset; and limiting access to the digital asset only from the stored geolocation coordinates of the authorized device, and only at the stored authorized times of operation. 6. The method of claim 1, and registering a name of the user with the digital asset when both the scanned key data matches the stored key data, and when the sent device data matches the stored device identifier. 7. The method of claim 1, wherein the object is a card with the authentication key, and registering the card to uniquely identify the user when both the scanned key data matches the stored key data, and when the sent device data matches the stored device identifier. 8. The method of claim 1, wherein the object is a storage medium that stores a private key, and allowing access to the private key when both the scanned key data matches the stored key data, and when the sent device data matches the stored device identifier. 9. The method of claim 1, and displaying a plurality of icons indicative of at least one of a card, a service, an asset on the device, and activating a selected icon when bom the scanned key data matches the stored key data, and when the sent device data matches the stored device identifier. 10. The method of claim 1, and authorizing at least one additional mobile device to read the authentication key; storing an additional device identifier indicative of the at least one additional authorized device in the database; sending additional device data indicative of the at least one additional authorized device that is scanning the authentication key to the database; determining if the sent additional device data matches the additional stored device identifier; and allowing access to the digital asset when both the sent device data matches the stored device identifier, and when the sent additional device data matches the additional stored device identifier. 11. The method of claim 10, and configuring the tag with an additional authentication key that uniquely identifies the object; storing additional key data indicative of 

 the additional authentication key in the database; and scanning each authentication key with either authorized device in response to a prompt from the digital asset. 12. The method of claim 1, wherein the machine-readable data of the second dataset is encoded in at least one radio frequency identification (RFID) chip.
